73314.name: Settler Intelligence Briefing
73314.description:
Hostile rogue drones hunt and scavenge every system in this constellation we call Phoenix. They grow stronger as you move away from Angelice. They are reported more commonly near planets but sightings occur almost anywhere.

Large asteroid clusters have been cataloged in each system. In some, a large rogue drone structure appears to be devouring the raw ore and so the Settlers have begun calling the clusters 'Moon Eater'. Another location with dense resources has been found, but exploration attempts have failed due to an infested Abaddon who sits on watch. Ophidia Operations, based in Serpentis, are working on a technology to provide concealment.

Your starter pack contains a blueprint we believe will provide access to the system Askur, which is currently locked by sophisticated encryption. We don't know what Askur has in store.

Settler security forces have not yet been established, guard yourself. However, should your ship be destroyed, starter packs can be purchased from the market here in Angelice.

77484.name: Crude Extractor
77484.description: Module used in the extraction of crude matter required for all types of fuel by drawing exotic material through a crude rift.

77518.name: Lens 3X
77518.description: For use with Crude Extractors only.

77729.name: Rough Old Crude Matter
77729.description:
Braids of serenity and power entwine and disperse across the surface. Within, they dance as if begging to be set free. From this rhythm, the dreams of those long passed and those newly awoken may spring forth.

A quiet fragility eminates from the matter’s movements, the ethereal beauty of transience. Practicality has no need of such things.

77753.name: Embark
77753.description: Extra small hydrogen engine. Runs exclusively on hydrogen fuel.

77800.name: Feldspar Crystals
77800.description:
Calcium-sodium aluminosilicate feldspar crystals from basaltic crust of differentiated asteroids.

Feldspar Crystals are commonly found in Char asteroids.

77801.name: Nickel-Iron Veins
77801.description:
Nickel-bearing alloys commonly intermixed with iron in metallic asteroids. 

Nickel-Iron Veins is commonly refined from Platinum-Palladium Matrix.

77803.name: Silicon Dust
77803.description:
Abundant non-metal hosted in silicate minerals throughout asteroids. 

Silicon Dust is commonly refined from Silica Grains.

77804.name: Tholin Aggregates
77804.description:
Prebiotic tar-like organics produced by long-term radiation chemistry. 

Tholin Aggregates is commonly refined from Hydrocarbon Residue.

77805.name: Platinum-Group Veins
77805.description:
Rare platinum-group metals concentrated in differentiated asteroid cores. 

Platinum-Group Veins is commonly refined from Platinum-Palladium Matrix.

77810.name: Platinum-Palladium Matrix
77810.description:
Platinum and palladium concentrated in nickel-rich metallic grains from intermediate core zones. Forms during slow cooling of differentiated asteroid cores.

Platinum-Palladium Matrix is commonly found in Slag asteroids.

77811.name: Hydrated Sulfide Matrix
77811.description:
Water-bearing sulfide-rich material common in carbonaceous chondrites. Represents aqueous alteration of metal sulfides in the early solar system.

Hydrated Sulfide Matrix is commonly found in Comet asteroids.

77818.name: Unstable Fuel
77818.description: Long abandoned and left to the mercy of the Static Shear, little energy or even structure remains. This lingering dross provides barely enough of a spark to power a vessel. Even the best refinery processes cannot prevent the decay of time from claiming their product.

77852.name: Small Cutting Laser
77852.description: A direct energy tool useful for breaking apart solid matter. Like many of its forebearers, it can serve needs beyond its intended purpose. Adaptation is necessary for survival.

78423.name: Water Ice
78423.description:
Essential volatile preserved in cold outer-belt environments. 

Hydrated Sulfide Matrix and Methane Ice Shards are common sources of Water Ice.

78426.name: Iridosmine Nodules
78426.description:
Dense iridium-osmium-ruthenium alloy nodules from the deepest, hottest core regions. Among the most refractory materials in the solar system.

Iridosmine Nodules are commonly found in Ingot asteroids.

78434.name: Rough Young Crude Matter
78434.description:
Currents of energy flicker almost playfully just beneath the surface. Deeper, the flows meld, forming a sea of emotion. The hue surrounding the matter changes with these internal tides, reflecting whichever passion dominates in the moment.

It is this mercurial nature which entices, which promises the ability to shape the future. So sweet is the call that danger, no matter how apparent, is easily ignored.

78446.name: Methane Ice Shards
78446.description:
Pure methane ice preserved at cryogenic temperatures in outer asteroid belt regions.

Methane Ice Shards are commonly found in Dewdrop asteroids.

78447.name: Primitive Kerogen Matrix
78447.description:
Unaltered complex organic polymer preserved from early solar system formation, less processed than C-type kerogen.

Primitive Kerogen Matrix is commonly found in Ember asteroids.

78448.name: Aromatic Carbon Veins
78448.description:
Concentrated deposits of complex aromatic hydrocarbons formed through extended space weathering and radiation processing of organic ices.

Aromatic Carbon Veins are commonly found in Glint asteroids.

78449.name: Tholin Nodules
78449.description:
Prebiotic tar-like organics formed from radiation-driven chemistry.

Tholin Nodules are commonly found in Soot asteroids.

78490.name: Velocity CD81
78490.description: Engine with high performance and high power requirments. Requires any kind of refined Crude fuel such as EU-40 to operate. Will not function using hydrogen fuel.

78502.name: Velocity CD82
78502.description: Engine with high performance and high power requirments. Requires any kind of refined Crude fuel such as EU-40 to operate. Will not function using hydrogen fuel.

78504.name: Tempo CD43
78504.description: Engine with a balance of performance and power requirements. Requires any kind of refined Crude fuel such as EU-40 to operate. Will not function using hydrogen fuel.

78506.name: Celerity CD01
78506.description: Engine with low performance but low power requirements. Requires any kind of refined Crude fuel such as EU-40 to operate. Will not function using hydrogen fuel.

78507.name: Celerity CD02
78507.description: Engine with low performance but low power requirements. Requires any kind of refined Crude fuel such as EU-40 to operate. Will not function using hydrogen fuel.

78508.name: Celerity CD03
78508.description: Engine with low performance but low power requirements. Requires any kind of refined Crude fuel such as EU-40 to operate. Will not function using hydrogen fuel.

78510.name: Tempo CD42
78510.description: Engine with a balance of performance and power requirements. Requires any kind of refined Crude fuel such as EU-40 to operate. Will not function using hydrogen fuel.

78511.name: Tempo CD41
78511.description: Engine with a balance of performance and power requirements. Requires any kind of refined Crude fuel such as EU-40 to operate. Will not function using hydrogen fuel.

81609.name: USV
81609.description: A light vessel optimized for resource extraction (placeholder).

81611.name: Chumaq
81611.description: A large hauler with extensive cargo capacity. (placeholder)

81808.name: TADES
81808.description: A medium-sized and flexible combat vessel. (placeholder)

81846.name: Sojourn
81846.description: Small hydrogen engine. Runs exclusively on hydrogen fuel.

81904.name: MCF
81904.description: A lightweight combat vessel designed focused on evading fire. (placeholder)

82424.name: HAF
82424.description: A small but durable combat vessel. (placeholder)

82425.name: LAI
82425.description: An ultra-lightweight combat vessel designed to focus on striking fast. (placeholder)

82426.name: LORHA
82426.description: A small hauler designed to travel through combat zones. (placeholder)

82667.name: Shield Restorer II
82667.description: Improves the recharge rate of the shield.

82682.name: Warp Entangler II
82682.description: Momentarily prevents a ship from warping.

82683.name: Stasis Net II
82683.description: Momentarily reduces the maximum speed of a ship.

83458.name: Shield Restorer III
83458.description: Improves the recharge rate of the shield.

83459.name: Shield Restorer IV
83459.description: Improves the recharge rate of the shield.

83516.name: Warp Entangler III
83516.description: Momentarily prevents a ship from warping. ( copy )

83517.name: Warp Entangler IV
83517.description: Momentarily prevents a ship from warping. ( copy )

83518.name: Warp Entangler V
83518.description: Momentarily prevents a ship from warping. ( copy )

83519.name: Warp Entangler VI
83519.description: Momentarily prevents a ship from warping. ( copy )

83520.name: Stasis Net III
83520.description: Momentarily reduces the maximum speed of a ship. ( copy )

83521.name: Stasis Net IV
83521.description: Momentarily reduces the maximum speed of a ship. ( copy )

83522.name: Stasis Net V
83522.description: Momentarily reduces the maximum speed of a ship. ( copy )

83523.name: Stasis Net VI
83523.description: Momentarily reduces the maximum speed of a ship. ( copy )

83525.name: Purified Moon Cutting Laser
83525.description: The violence necessary to obtain such a trophy has reduced its functionality to a fraction of its potential. Yet, this laser remains operational in the face of all the Frontier’s hostilities, natural and computational alike. Fitting a feralized component, even one with its protocols exorcised and rebuilt, can be considered an act of hubris.

83528.name: Medium Cutting Laser
83528.description: A direct energy tool useful for breaking apart solid matter. Like many of its forebearers, it can serve needs beyond its intended purpose. Flexibility has served the denizens of the Frontier well.

83818.name: Fossilized Exotronics
83818.description: The Null and Static Shears wreak havoc on most traditional forms of computation, rendering them nigh unusable in the Frontier. Exotronic technology's extreme adaptability and durability thus effectively make it the only option for automation in this environment. Faint remnants of activity in this debris suggest the potential for reclamation. Within these dull impulses of fragmented information, a hint of familiarity calls out.

84180.name: Printed Circuits
84180.description: Cheap and modular basic electronics computing system.

84182.name: Reinforced Alloys
84182.description: A frontier standard alloy with versatile use cases.

84210.name: Carbon Weave
84210.description: Strong material for products requiring lower weight.

84556.name: Smart Turret
84556.description: A structure with a turret slot which you can equip with any turret.

84955.name: Heavy Gate
84955.description: Establish your own stargate by connecting two long distance Smart Gates together.

87120.name: Heavy Printer
87120.description: Industrial printer that handles materials in large quantities.

87160.name: Refuge
87160.description: Offers a respite after a long journey. Offers fitting services.

87161.name: Field Refinery
87161.description: Cheap and portable refinery that goes anywhere with you.

87162.name: Field Printer
87162.description: Portable printer that can produce basic survival items.

87566.name: Field Storage
87566.description: A small storage unit to store your resources.

87698.name: Wend
87698.description: A vessel able to complete basic tasks. Provided by Keep as a springboard for Project Awakening (placeholder)

87846.name: Recurve
87846.description: A small hard hitting combat vessel emphasizing firepower over hull strength. (Placeholder)

87847.name: Reflex
87847.description:
Created for efficient, long distance travel. Records suggest the structure of this vessel has been repurposed numerous times as automated processes sought to adapt to an increasingly hostile environment. 

As a result, the original intention behind this hull design has been lost to the millennia and data corruption has obscured even whether this design originates from a human or artificial mind. However, the data streams which led to this vessel's development have their origins in the K4T-Y region. (placeholder)

88063.name: Refinery
88063.description: A refinery capable of extracting main minerals from all ores.

88064.name: Heavy Refinery
88064.description: Industrial refinery capable of extracting most of the minerals from all ores.

88067.name: Printer
88067.description: A printer that handles materials in bulk.

88068.name: Assembler
88068.description: Assembles materials into end products like modules.

88069.name: Mini Berth
88069.description: Small shipyard for building shuttles and corvettes.

88070.name: Berth
88070.description: A shipyard for building frigates, destroyers and cruisers.

88071.name: Heavy Berth
88071.description: A large shipyard for building battleships and battlecruisers.

88086.name: Mini Gate
88086.description: Establish your own stargate by connecting two short range Small Gates together.

88092.name: Network Node
88092.description:
Establish your base with a Network Node. Stabilizes and fuels other structures in its range. Must be placed at an unclaimed Anchor point.

Once built you can set it as a home base.

88093.name: Shelter
88093.description: A hangar for ship sizes up to and including frigates.

88094.name: Heavy Shelter
88094.description: A large hangar for ship sizes up to and including battlecruisers.

88100.name: Wall 1
88100.description: A large structure used to create a physical obstacle for defensive purposes.

88101.name: Wall 2
88101.description: A large structure used to create a physical obstacle for defensive purposes.

88234.name: Troilite Sulfide Grains
88234.description:
Iron sulfide mineral characteristic of primitive carbonaceous asteroids. 

Hydrated Sulfide Matrix is a common source of Troilite Sulfide Grains.

88235.name: Feldspar Crystal Shards
88235.description:
Fractured aluminum tectosilicate minerals common in rocky and differentiated asteroids, typically formed through magmatic cooling processes.

Feldspar Crystal Shards are commonly refined from Feldspar Crystals.

88319.name: D2 Fuel
88319.description: Even the most mundane matter can be improved through synthesis with byproducts of Crude extraction. Plentiful and inefficient on the Frontier, hydrogen too may be uplifted through this process, breathing new life into reactors which would otherwise be left behind. A question remains: are these efforts efficient?

88335.name: D1 Fuel
88335.description: The allure of Crude Matter brought computation and humanity to the Frontier. As life reawakens, so too does the thirst for power in all its forms, including from the most mundane sources. Even in the face of the Static Shear, ice still litters the heavens. From its crystals, hydrogen may be extracted, reprocessed, and refined into fuel. This product, inefficient as it is, still drives the smallest vessels of the Frontier via its abundance and minimal cost.

88561.name: Thermal Composites
88561.description: Layered ceramics and metals that results in heat resistant manufacturing material.

88765.name: Mummified Clone
88765.description: An air of reverence lingers around this corpse. Marks of age and violence cannot fully conceal the care given to this body, long abandoned by its original owner. The time for remembrance, however, has passed.

88780.name: Echo Chamber
88780.description: Strong material for products requiring lower weight. ( copy )

88781.name: Chitinous Organics
88781.description:
Carbon-based polymer residues structurally analogous to chitin, indicative of complex prebiotic or biologically processed compounds.

Aromatic Carbon Veins, Methane Ice Shards, Primitive Kerogen Matrix and Tholin Nodules are a common source of Chitinous Organics.

88782.name: Aromatic Carbon Weave
88782.description:
Polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons formed by radiation processing of carbon-rich ices. 

Aromatic Carbon Veins is a common source of Aromatic Carbon Weave.

88783.name: Kerogen Tar
88783.description:
Complex organic polymer preserved from the early solar system. 

Kerogen Tar is commonly refined from Primitive Kerogen Matrix.

88839.name: Batched Printed Circuits
88839.description: Cheap and modular basic electronics computing system. ( copy )

88840.name: Packaged Printed Circuits
88840.description: Cheap and modular basic electronics computing system. ( copy )

88841.name: Batched Carbon Weave
88841.description: Strong material for products requiring lower weight. ( copy )

88842.name: Packaged Carbon Weave
88842.description: Strong material for products requiring lower weight. ( copy )

88843.name: Batched Thermal Composites
88843.description: Layered ceramics and metals that results in heat resistant manufacturing material. ( copy )

88844.name: Packaged Thermal Composites
88844.description: Layered ceramics and metals that results in heat resistant manufacturing material. ( copy )

88886.name: Heat Exchanger XS
88886.description: Increases the rate at which internal temperature reaches equilibrium with external temperature

88889.name: Heat Exchanger S
88889.description: Increases the rate at which internal temperature reaches equilibrium with external temperature

89258.name: Hydrocarbon Residue
89258.description:
Complex organic compounds formed from carbonaceous ices and dust. 

Tholin Nodules and Aromatic Carbon Veins are common sources of Hydrocarbon Residue.

89259.name: Silica Grains
89259.description:
Silicon dioxide grains that dominate many stony asteroid crusts. 

Silica Grains is commonly refined from Feldspar Crystals.

89260.name: Iron-Rich Nodules
89260.description:
Most common structural metal in asteroids. 

Platinum-Palladium Matrix is a common source of Iron-Rich Nodules.

89775.name: SEER I
89775.description: Sate the hunger for the arcane. This monolith resonates with long-forgotten knowledge.

89776.name: SEER II
89776.description: Sate the hunger for the arcane. This monolith resonates with long-forgotten knowledge.

89777.name: HARBINGER I
89777.description: Gnaw on the bones of the lost world. Monuments like this one have once sat at the eyes of the Feral storms.

89778.name: HARBINGER II
89778.description: Gnaw on the bones of the lost world. Monuments like this one have once sat at the eyes of the Feral storms.

89779.name: RAINMAKER II
89779.description: Follow the slipstream towards the source of world's blood. This sculpture is imprinted with Frontier's primal forces.

89780.name: RAINMAKER I
89780.description: Follow the slipstream towards the source of world's blood. This sculpture is imprinted with Frontier's primal forces.

89794.name: Defiled Crude Matter
89794.description:
Human greed carves gashes into the surface. Past sins bind to the structures within, transforming the promised bounty into a twisted relic. It enters this universe broken and scarred, carrying only a fraction of its potential.

Yet it is still Crude. It can still rouse dreams thought long extinct. Perhaps even the accursed dream responsible for its creation.

90184.name: Relay
90184.description: Establishes a connection to EVE Net, allowing the purchase of data, thus enabling the fabrication Crude mining lenses, as well as the sale of feral data.

90887.name: Hop
90887.description:
A transit module for covering ground. Once engaged, the vessel holds its heading and is carried forward until the burn completes. An ancient choice for hopping between adjacent points of interest.

Among the oldest schemata found in the Frontier. It is simulated that countless variants have descended from a theorised original pattern, each adapted to different hulls and purposes, yet the core principle remains unchanged. The first makers understood something fundamental about motion that later work has only decorated.

91106.name: Stride
91106.description:
A frame stripped for range, built to leap between stars.
The design bears apparent alterations — conduits rerouted through spaces that seem improvised, geometries bent into configurations that defy intuition.
No signature remains to mark its maker.

91107.name: Carom
91107.description: A hull shaped for distance and solitude. The proportions are spare, stripped of anything not essential to the crossing. The drive assembly carries unexpected mass — bracing layered over bracing, housings reinforced beyond apparent need, structure filling spaces the original geometry left hollow. What necessity shaped these adaptations remains unclear.

91205.name: Redundant Module
91205.description: A fragment of endless growth, stripped of purpose yet stubbornly persistent.

91206.name: Void Residue
91206.description: A lingering trace of chaotic growth, the remnants of energy that once fueled a self-propagating anomaly.

91209.name: Singularity Reactor Fragment
91209.description: Small but potent, it pulses with the force that drives endless growth.

91210.name: Void fragments
91210.description: Shards born from the collapse of unstable matter, whispering of relentless expansion and unseen forces.

91374.name: Char
91374.description:
Charred remnants of volcanic crust, these fire-scarred asteroids contain crystalline feldspar minerals forged in the intense heat of differentiated parent bodies. Their burnt, weathered surfaces hide valuable aluminum and silicon-rich crystals beneath layers of space-weathered rock.

Char asteroids are a common source of Feldspar Crystals and can typically be found in Inner Belts.

91375.name: Slag
91375.description:
Industrial waste from the cosmos—metallic slag asteroids are the leftover cores of shattered worlds. Rich in platinum and palladium, these slowly-cooled metallic matrices represent the most precious remnants of planetary formation, concentrated in dense nickel-rich grains.

Slag asteroids are a common source of Platinum-Palladium Matrix and can typically be found in Inner Belts.

91376.name: Ingot
91376.description:
Pure metallic ingots drifting through space, these asteroids are nature's own refined metal bars. Containing the densest materials known—iridium, osmium, and ruthenium—they're the treasure vaults of the asteroid belt, forged in the deepest, hottest cores of primordial worlds.

Ingot asteroids are a common source of Iridosmine Nodules.

91377.name: Comet
91377.description:
Primitive water-bearers from stellar formation, these comet-like asteroids hold preserved secrets in their hydrated sulfide cores. Part ice, part metal, they're time capsules of aqueous chemistry now frozen in the void.

Comet asteroids are a common source of Hydrated Sulfide Matrix and can typically be found in Outer Belts.

91378.name: Dewdrop
91378.description:
Delicate spheres of frozen volatiles, dewdrop asteroids glisten with crystalline methane ice preserved at near-absolute-zero temperatures. These fragile outer belt wanderers are reservoirs of pure cryogenic fuel, waiting to be harvested from the cold reaches of space.

Dewdrop asteroids are a common source of Methane Ice Shards.

91379.name: Ember
91379.description:
Still glowing with primordial organic heat, ember asteroids contain pristine kerogen—complex carbon polymers that have remained unaltered since formation. These dark, coal-like bodies hold valuable chemical building blocks.

Ember asteroids are a common source of Primitive Kerogen Matrix.

91380.name: Glint
91380.description:
Shimmering with rainbow-like iridescence, glint asteroids sparkle with veins of complex aromatic hydrocarbons. Radiation-forged through extended exposure, their surfaces gleam with polycyclic organic compounds—the cosmic equivalent of liquid crystal displays, beautiful and chemically exotic.

Glint asteroids are a common source of Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbon Veins.

91381.name: Soot
91381.description:
Dark as coal dust and deeply primordial, soot asteroids are coated in prebiotic tholin nodules—tar-like organic compounds formed through radiation chemistry. These sooty spheres are laboratories of cosmic chemistry, their surfaces slowly processing in the radiation of deep space.

Soot asteroids are a common source of Tholin Nodules.

91701.name: Printer
91701.description: A printer that handles materials in bulk.

91702.name: Heavy Printer
91702.description: Industrial printer that handles materials in large quantities.

91703.name: Refinery
91703.description: A refinery capable of extracting main minerals from all ores.

91704.name: Heavy Refinery
91704.description: Industrial refinery capable of extracting most of the minerals from all ores.

91705.name: Mini Berth
91705.description: Small shipyard for building shuttles and corvettes.

91706.name: Berth
91706.description: A shipyard for building frigates, destroyers and cruisers.

91707.name: Heavy Berth
91707.description: A large shipyard for building battleships and battlecruisers.

91708.name: Assembler
91708.description: Assembles materials into end products like modules.

91709.name: Shelter
91709.description: A hangar for ship sizes up to and including frigates.

91710.name: Heavy Shelter
91710.description: A large hangar for ship sizes up to and including battlecruisers.

91711.name: Mini Gate
91711.description: Establish your own stargate by connecting two short range Small Gates together.

91712.name: Heavy Gate
91712.description: Establish your own stargate by connecting two long distance Smart Gates together.

91717.name: Relay
91717.description: Establishes a connection to EVE Net, allowing the purchase of data, thus enabling the fabrication Crude mining lenses, as well as the sale of feral data.

91720.name: Wall 1
91720.description: A large structure used to create a physical obstacle for defensive purposes.

91721.name: Wall 2
91721.description: A large structure used to create a physical obstacle for defensive purposes.

91722.name: RAINMAKER I
91722.description: Follow the slipstream towards the source of world's blood. This sculpture is imprinted with Frontier's primal forces.

91723.name: RAINMAKER II
91723.description: Follow the slipstream towards the source of world's blood. This sculpture is imprinted with Frontier's primal forces.

91724.name: HARBINGER I
91724.description: Gnaw on the bones of the lost world. Monuments like this one have once sat at the eyes of the Feral storms.

91725.name: HARBINGER II
91725.description: Gnaw on the bones of the lost world. Monuments like this one have once sat at the eyes of the Feral storms.

91726.name: SEER I
91726.description: Sate the hunger for the arcane. This monolith resonates with long-forgotten knowledge.

91727.name: SEER II
91727.description: Sate the hunger for the arcane. This monolith resonates with long-forgotten knowledge.

91751.name: Refuge
91751.description: Offers a respite after a long journey. Offers fitting services.

91752.name: Field Refinery
91752.description: Cheap and portable refinery that goes anywhere with you.

91753.name: Field Printer
91753.description: Portable printer that can produce basic survival items.

91756.name: Field Storage
91756.description: A small storage unit to store your resources.

91871.name: Nest
91871.description: A facility for storing Shells an transferring between them.

91937.name: Calamitous Marrow
91937.description: Chaos permeates the Frontier at all scales, converting stabilizing forces into agents of transformation. The evolutionary havoc pulsing through the trabeculae of this substance tantalizes with the possibilities it may unlock. Its desirable qualities, in turn, ensure its rarity, for the conditions which lead to its creation are impossible to replicate.

91978.name: Nursery
91978.description: A facility for growing synthetic Shells.

91997.name: Lens 9001X
91997.description: For use with Crude Extractors only.

92165.name: Nursery
92165.description: A facility for growing synthetic Shells.

92166.name: Nest
92166.description: A facility for storing Shells an transferring between them.

92279.name: Mini Turret
92279.description: A small base defence turret. Especially effective against smaller targets.

92280.name: Mini Turret
92280.description: A small base defence turret. Especially effective against smaller targets.

92389.name: Skip
92389.description:
A transit module for covering ground. Once engaged, the vessel holds its heading and is carried forward until the burn completes. An ancient choice for hopping between adjacent points of interest.

Among the oldest schemata found in the Frontier. It is simulated that countless variants have descended from a theorised original pattern, each adapted to different hulls and purposes, yet the core principle remains unchanged. The first makers understood something fundamental about motion that later work has only decorated. ( copy )

92390.name: Lunge
92390.description:
A transit module for covering ground. Once engaged, the vessel holds its heading and is carried forward until the burn completes. An ancient choice for hopping between adjacent points of interest.

Among the oldest schemata found in the Frontier. It is simulated that countless variants have descended from a theorised original pattern, each adapted to different hulls and purposes, yet the core principle remains unchanged. The first makers understood something fundamental about motion that later work has only decorated. ( copy )

92394.name: Fine Young Crude Matter
92394.description:
Tight weaves of energetic currents tangle and dissipate beneath the surface. Deeper, they merge so seamlessly as to appear singular in nature, concealing the true chaos seething within. The hue surrounding the matter subtly reflects this hidden truth, oscillating wildly between wave forms.

It is chaos which offers potential, enticing all who might stumble upon its power. So tempting is the possibility that risks may be easily ignored.

92401.name: Turret
92401.description: A medium base defence turret. Especially effective against mid sized ships.

92404.name: Heavy Turret
92404.description: A large base defence turret. Especially effective against larger ships.

92406.name: Turret
92406.description: A medium base defence turret. Especially effective against mid sized ships.

92407.name: Heavy Turret
92407.description: A large base defence turret. Especially effective against larger ships.

92414.name: Fine Old Crude Matter
92414.description:
Dense braids of tranquil, potent energy dance across the surface. Within, they ply together as if seeking to become one. From this unmet desire, the fuel of dreams might be extracted, as practiced by those long passed.

The ethereal beauty of transience emanates from compacting, ever-shifting weaves of sinew. Appreciation goes beyond immediate need and can therefore be discarded.

92421.name: Leap
92421.description:
A transit module for covering ground. Once engaged, the vessel holds its heading and is carried forward until the burn completes. An ancient choice for hopping between adjacent points of interest.

Among the oldest schemata found in the Frontier. It is simulated that countless variants have descended from a theorised original pattern, each adapted to different hulls and purposes, yet the core principle remains unchanged. The first makers understood something fundamental about motion that later work has only decorated. ( copy ) ( copy )

99001.name: Palladium
99001.description:
A rare platinum-group metal used in high-grade alloys and electronics.

Platinum-Palladium Matrix is a common source of Palladium.
